message:
perf: cache tool defs, prompt components, reduce history copies

Three optimizations to the processOneInput() hot loop:

1. Tool definitions cached at init via rebuildToolDefsCache(). Two
   pre-built lists (with/without result tool) swap based on
   MinResultRound gate. Cache invalidated on RegisterTool() and
   subagent tool restriction.

2. History copies reduced from 3 to 1 per round. Context management
   and message expansion share a single copy. AfterAction receives
   s.history directly (read-only — verified it never mutates).

3. System prompt components cached at init via rebuildPromptCache():
   skillList, extraTools string, agentSection, nonInteractiveGuidance.
   Only BuildSystemPrompt() is called per-round with cached inputs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -134,6 +134,9 @@ func TestMCPIntegration_ToolCallThroughSession(t *testing.T) {
 	}
 	sess.mcpMgr = mgr
 	sess.mcpTools = mgr.ToolDefinitions()
+	// Rebuild caches to pick up the MCP tools added after session creation.
+	sess.rebuildToolDefsCache()
+	sess.rebuildPromptCache()
 
 	tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 10*time.Second)
 	defer cancel()

@@ -158,6 +158,8 @@ func (s *Session) spawnAgent(ctx context.Context, task, model, workingDir string
 		subSess.reg.Remove("wait")
 		subSess.reg.Remove("close_agent")
 	}
+	// Rebuild cached tool definitions after restriction/removal.
+	subSess.rebuildToolDefsCache()
 
 	sub := &subagent{
 		id:           subSess.id,
